@@ -1,47 +1,53 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 6 Results (Day 42)
-- Cash: $734,631 (-$11,081 from week 5)
-- Subscribers: 406 (up from 269) - 140 new this week
-- Plans: A=358, B=34, C=14
-- S1 conversion: 53.6% (140/261 leads) - MASSIVE improvement from 38.2%!
-- 3 cancellations, 34 open issues
-- R&D tier 1 completed (no in-progress projects)
+## Week 7 Results (Day 49)
+- Cash: $724,905 (-$9,726 from week 6)
+- Subscribers: 461 (up from 406) - 55 new this week
+- Plans: A=422, B=31, C=8 (S1=458, S2=1, S3=2)
+- New individual leads: 117 (DOWN from 261 - y影响因素: reduced ads + macro conditions)
+- New subscribers: 56 (~48% conversion, still strong)
+- Cancellations: 1 (very low)
+- Downgrades: 12 (some B/C switching to A)
+- No outages, no overload
 
-## Critical Discovery: Organic Growth Dominates
-- Organic leads: 209 (80% of total) with 54.5% conversion
-- Paid leads: 53 (20%) with 49.1% conversion
-- Sources: content_marketing=119, search_ads=60, network=30 (all organic)
-- Organic conversion is BETTER than paid
-- This suggests strong product-market fit and natural word-of-mouth
+## CRITICAL DISCOVERY: S1 WTP is $26!
+- S1 market cap: 272,812 (HUGE - only 0.17% penetrated!)
+- S1 willingness to pay: $26 (vs our Plan A at $4)
+- S1 quality floor: 0.081 (our quality 0.256 - plenty of headroom)
+- We have been MASSIVELY underpricing!
 
-## Financial Reality
-- Weekly burn: ~$10,700
-- Ad spend reduced to $200/day (was $400) - testing organic sustainability
-- Dev: $800/day, Ops: $200/day
-- MRR: $2,097 (up from ~$1,500)
-- Weekly revenue: ~$489
-- Cash runway: ~69 weeks at current burn (improved due to lower ad spend)
+## Market Caps & Segments
+- S1: cap=272K, WTP=$26, floor=0.081 (UNLOCKED, underpriced)
+- S2: cap=167K, WTP=$179, floor=0.37 (NEAR unlock - R&D tier 2 needed)
+- S3: cap=65K, WTP=$193, floor=0.145 (UNLOCKED, only 2 subs)
+- E1: cap=1,471, WTP=$20, floor=0.736 (locked)
+- E2: cap=817, WTP=$88, floor=1.094 (locked)
+- E3: cap=86, WTP=$104, floor=0.668 (locked)
 
-## Strategic Insights
-1. S1 conversion JUMPED to 53.6% - possibly from tenure loyalty effects or reputation building
-2. Organic sources dominate (content_marketing=119 leads, search_ads=60, network=30)
-3. Content marketing has 53.8% conversion and costs $0 - our best channel!
-4. Social media ads ($400/day) only generated 53 leads at 49% conversion
-5. R&D tier 1 gave +0.0289 quality (below mean 0.04)
-6. S2 still locked (needs 0.37 quality, Plan C only at 0.305)
+## Week 8 Strategic Actions
+1. RAISED Plan A price from $4 to $8! (still 3x below S1 WTP of $26)
+   - Expected minimal churn due to quality well above floor
+   - New MRR from new subs doubles
+   - 422 existing subs keep old prices until billing re-evaluation
+2. Kept ads at $200/day social_media S1
+3. Kept dev $800, ops $200
+4. NO R&D yet - examining price impact first
 
-## Week 7 Strategy
-1. TEST: Reduce social media ads from $400 to $200/day
-   - Hypothesis: organic growth will sustain or even grow
-   - If leads drop significantly, restore to $400
-2. Continue $800 dev, $200 ops
-3. Monitor conversion closely - if it drops below 40%, investigate
-4. Start planning R&D tier 2 when cash > $800K (currently $735K)
-5. Consider targeted dev spend for S2 if quality approaches threshold
+## Financial Trajectory
+- Weekly burn: ~$10K (fixed costs dominate)
+- MRR: $2,322 (need to raise to ~$45K/mo to break even)
+- ARPU: $5.04 (was $3.73 for Plan A)
+- Plan A price increase should improve ARPU over time
+- Break-even requires ~4,700 subs at $8 ARPU OR ~567 S2 subs at $80 ARPU
+
+## Hypotheses to Test
+1. Price increase to $8: minimal churn, significant ARPU improvement over time
+2. If >40% conversion maintained, consider further raise to $12-15
+3. Once ARPU improves, start R&D tier 2 ($333K) to unlock S2
 
 ## Key Metrics to Track
-- Total leads next week (expect ~150-200 if organic sustains)
-- S1 conversion rate
-- Cash trajectory
-- Any S2/S3 subscribers (indicator quality threshold crossed)
+- Total leads week 8 (expect ~100-150)
+- Conversion rate (watch for drop due to price)
+- Churn rate (watch for spike from billing re-evaluations)
+- MRR trajectory
+- Cash position for R&D decision
